security_findings.entry_point: FltSendMessage to the App-V Streaming minifilter communication port (FilterSendMessage user-mode API)
security_findings.vulnerability_class: Design note: per-message authorization is not re-checked inside the dispatcher; authorization is enforced at the port (Administrators/SYSTEM only). Not a reachable authorization gap. Unchanged between builds.
security_findings.description: The function-level comparison reports ALL 274 functions as identical (similarity 1.0) with zero changed functions, and the disassembly and decompilation of the two files are byte-identical. The .text section (file offsets 0x400-0x14400) compares byte-for-byte equal, as do .data, .pdata, .idata and .reloc. The 0.993 overall similarity comes from build metadata only: the COFF link timestamp (file offset 0xE8), the .rdata debug directory (new CodeView/PDB GUID and updated debug timestamps; PDB name Appvstrm.pdb unchanged), the .rsrc version resource (FileVersion/ProductVersion advance from 10.0.17763.3469 to 10.0.17763.4010), and the PE Authenticode certificate directory (re-signed; countersignature timestamps advance from 2022 to 2023; timestamp-service certificate organization changes from 'Microsoft Operations Puerto Rico' to 'Microsoft Ireland Operations Limited'; ASCII string count 1711 -> 1714). This is a code-identical rebuild with a version bump and re-sign; appvstrm.sys has no CVE attribution in this update. The driver is a Windows kernel minifilter (Microsoft App-V Streaming Mini-Filter) that exposes staging operations to a connected client via FltSendMessage through a filter communication port. ProcessMessage (0x1C0012B80) dispatches on the leading DWORD of the input buffer via a decrement chain (cmp r8d,8; mov ecx,[rdx]; sub ecx,1; jz ...) to seven handled message types: 1=create_file (0x1C000FCB4), 2=write_file (0x1C000FE44), 3=request_mdl (0x1C0010404), 6=query_sparse_file (0x1C001059C), 7=create_hard_link (0x1C00106AC), 8=config (dispatch block 0x1C0012CB6, writes gStrmFltData+0x30), 9=set_directory_DACL (0x1C0010300). ProcessMessage does not re-check the caller per message. Authorization is enforced once, at the port: Streaming::Messaging::UserCommunication::Initialize (0x1C0012A80) requests DesiredAccess = FLT_PORT_ALL_ACCESS (0x1F0001, mov edx at 0x1C0012AA0), calls FltBuildDefaultSecurityDescriptor (0x1C0012AAC), and passes the descriptor to FltCreateCommunicationPort (0x1C0012B37). FltBuildDefaultSecurityDescriptor grants the requested access to Administrators and SYSTEM only, so non-privileged callers are denied at FilterConnectCommunicationPort. Because only Administrators/SYSTEM can reach the staging handlers, this dispatch design does not expose a missing-authorization primitive to unprivileged callers. The port names in the driver are \StrmFltCommunicationClientServerPort and \StrmFltCommunicationServerClientPort. Notable handlers: request_mdl (kernel MDL allocation via Streaming::MDLCache::GetFreeMdl), create_hard_link (calls Streaming::Messaging::PackageLoader::CreateHardLink at 0x1C0010790), set_directory_DACL (calls Streaming::DACLSetter::SetDirectoryDACL at 0x1C00103CA). This dispatch and port-init code is byte-identical in both builds; the update does not change it.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: Open a handle to the App-V Streaming minifilter communication port (\StrmFltCommunicationClientServerPort / \StrmFltCommunicationServerClientPort) via FilterConnectCommunicationPort, then send a FltSendMessage with message type 1/2/3/6/7/8/9. Whether a caller can connect is governed by the port security descriptor, which Initialize (0x1C0012A80) builds via FltBuildDefaultSecurityDescriptor with FLT_PORT_ALL_ACCESS. That descriptor grants access to Administrators and SYSTEM only; a non-admin caller is denied at connect time. The descriptor is byte-identical in both builds, so both behave the same. This update does not change the descriptor or the dispatcher.
security_findings.primitive: No privilege-escalation primitive for an unprivileged caller. The staging operations (hard link creation, DACL modification, file create/write) are reachable only by callers the port admits (Administrators and SYSTEM per FltBuildDefaultSecurityDescriptor). An Administrator/SYSTEM caller invoking these operations does not cross a privilege boundary. No memory-corruption primitive exists; the code is byte-identical to the prior build.
security_findings.evidence:
ProcessMessage dispatch (identical in both binaries, 0x1C0012B80):
0x1C0012BB4 : cmp r8d, 8 ; 0x1C0012BB8 jnb loc_1C0012BE5 (size gate)
0x1C0012BE5 : mov ecx, [rdx]  // message type from input buffer
0x1C0012BE7 : sub ecx, 1 ; 0x1C0012BEA jz loc_1C0012E8D -> create_file (type 1, 0x1C000FCB4, call @ 0x1C0012F1D)
0x1C0012BF0 : sub ecx, 1 ; 0x1C0012BF3 jz loc_1C0012D7A -> write_file (type 2, 0x1C000FE44, call @ 0x1C0012E0A)
0x1C0012BF9 : sub ecx, 1 ; 0x1C0012BFC jz loc_1C0012D5C -> request_mdl (type 3, 0x1C0010404, call @ 0x1C0012D6E)
0x1C0012C02 : sub ecx, 3 ; 0x1C0012C05 jz loc_1C0012D10 -> query_sparse_file (type 6, 0x1C001059C, call @ 0x1C0012D3C)
0x1C0012C0B : sub ecx, 1 ; 0x1C0012C0E jz loc_1C0012CF7 -> create_hard_link (type 7, 0x1C00106AC, call @ 0x1C0012D09)
0x1C0012C14 : sub ecx, 1 ; 0x1C0012C17 jz loc_1C0012CB6 -> config (type 8, writes gStrmFltData+0x30)
0x1C0012C1D : cmp ecx, 1 ; 0x1C0012C20 jz loc_1C0012C9A -> set_directory_DACL (type 9, 0x1C0010300, call @ 0x1C0012CAC)

Port authorization (Initialize, 0x1C0012A80):
0x1C0012AA0 : mov edx, 1F0001h  // DesiredAccess = FLT_PORT_ALL_ACCESS
0x1C0012AAC : call FltBuildDefaultSecurityDescriptor  // grants Administrators + SYSTEM only
0x1C0012B37 : call FltCreateCommunicationPort

Note: The dispatcher does not re-check the caller per message, but the port descriptor already restricts access to Administrators/SYSTEM. This update changes only build metadata (timestamp, PDB GUID, version resource 10.0.17763.3469 -> 4010) and the Authenticode certificate, not the code or the descriptor.
security_findings.assembly:
ProcessMessage dispatch logic (identical in both binaries, 0x1C0012B80):
0x1C0012BB4 : cmp r8d, 8
0x1C0012BB8 : jnb loc_1C0012BE5
0x1C0012BE5 : mov ecx, [rdx]
0x1C0012BE7 : sub ecx,1 / 0x1C0012BEA jz loc_1C0012E8D (type 1 -> create_file)
0x1C0012BF0 : sub ecx,1 / 0x1C0012BF3 jz loc_1C0012D7A (type 2 -> write_file)
0x1C0012BF9 : sub ecx,1 / 0x1C0012BFC jz loc_1C0012D5C (type 3 -> request_mdl)
0x1C0012C02 : sub ecx,3 / 0x1C0012C05 jz loc_1C0012D10 (type 6 -> query_sparse_file)
0x1C0012C0B : sub ecx,1 / 0x1C0012C0E jz loc_1C0012CF7 (type 7 -> create_hard_link)
0x1C0012C14 : sub ecx,1 / 0x1C0012C17 jz loc_1C0012CB6 (type 8 -> config)
0x1C0012C1D : cmp ecx,1 / 0x1C0012C20 jz loc_1C0012C9A (type 9 -> set_directory_DACL)

breakpoints.why: Message dispatch. rcx=this, rdx=input buffer, r8d=input size, r9=output buffer, arg_20=output size, arg_28=bytes returned. The first DWORD at [rdx] is the message type (1,2,3,6,7,8,9). Minimum size gate cmp r8d,8 at 0x1C0012BB4.

Port creation. Builds the port security descriptor via FltBuildDefaultSecurityDescriptor (call 0x1C0012AAC, DesiredAccess 0x1F0001 set at 0x1C0012AA0) and passes it to FltCreateCommunicationPort (0x1C0012B37). This is the sole authorization gate; it restricts access to Administrators and SYSTEM.
Port message notification handler that calls ProcessMessage (call at 0x1C0012976). Inspect the call stack to confirm FltMgr!FltSendMessage -> NotifyPortMessage -> ProcessMessage.
Hard link creation handler (message type 7). Constructs source/target UNICODE_STRINGs from input and calls Streaming::Messaging::PackageLoader::CreateHardLink (call at 0x1C0010790).
DACL modification handler (message type 9). Calls Streaming::DACLSetter::SetDirectoryDACL (call at 0x1C00103CA).

watch: [rdx] at ProcessMessage entry = message type code (1=create, 2=write, 3=mdl, 6=query, 7=hardlink, 8=config, 9=DACL)
Communication port handle in user mode: obtained via FilterConnectCommunicationPort to \StrmFltCommunicationClientServerPort
Port security descriptor built via FltBuildDefaultSecurityDescriptor at 0x1C0012AAC (Administrators/SYSTEM only) - byte-identical in both builds
key_offsets: 0x1C0012BB4: cmp r8d, 8 - minimum message size gate
0x1C0012BE5: mov ecx, [rdx] - reads message type from input buffer
0x1C0010432: request_mdl size check cmp edx, 0Ch (minimum 12 bytes)
0x1C000FABB: GetFreeMdl size threshold cmp edx, 1000h (4KB MDL cache bucket)
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: 1. Connect to the App-V Streaming minifilter port (\StrmFltCommunicationClientServerPort) via FilterConnectCommunicationPort. Connection is governed by the port descriptor built with FltBuildDefaultSecurityDescriptor, which admits Administrators and SYSTEM only. 2. Construct a message buffer with a DWORD type code (1,2,3,6,7,8,9) followed by the operation-specific payload (total length >= 8). 3. Call FilterSendMessage with the port handle, buffer, and sizes.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: Both binaries behave identically because the code and the port security descriptor are byte-identical. A caller holding Administrators/SYSTEM connects and dispatches the staging operation; a non-privileged caller is denied at FilterConnectCommunicationPort. This update does not change the descriptor, so the observation is the same on both.
debugging_aids.notes: The diff found ZERO function-level code changes - all 274 functions are identical between patched and unpatched, and the disassembly and decompilation are byte-identical (.text compares byte-for-byte equal). The 0.993 overall similarity comes from build metadata only: the COFF link timestamp (0xE8), the .rdata debug directory (new PDB GUID; PDB name Appvstrm.pdb unchanged), the .rsrc version resource (10.0.17763.3469 -> 10.0.17763.4010), and the PE Authenticode certificate directory (re-signed; countersignature timestamps 2022 -> 2023; timestamp-cert org 'Microsoft Operations Puerto Rico' -> 'Microsoft Ireland Operations Limited'; ASCII string count 1711 -> 1714). No CVE attribution in this update. This finding is an informational, unchanged design note: message dispatch is authorized at the port (Administrators/SYSTEM via FltBuildDefaultSecurityDescriptor), not per message, and this is identical in both builds.

summary: The diff between appvstrm_unpatched.sys and appvstrm_patched.sys (Microsoft App-V Streaming minifilter driver) shows ZERO function-level code changes - all 274 functions match as identical, and the disassembly and decompilation are byte-identical, with the .text section comparing byte-for-byte equal. The 0.993 overall similarity is due to build metadata only: the COFF link timestamp, the .rdata debug directory (new PDB GUID), the .rsrc version resource (FileVersion/ProductVersion 10.0.17763.3469 -> 10.0.17763.4010), and the PE Authenticode certificate directory (re-signed; countersignature timestamps 2022 -> 2023; timestamp-cert org 'Microsoft Operations Puerto Rico' -> 'Microsoft Ireland Operations Limited'). This is a code-identical rebuild with a version bump and re-sign; appvstrm.sys has no CVE attribution and this pair contains no code-level security change. As an informational design note (unchanged between builds), the driver exposes staging operations (create_file 0x1C000FCB4, write_file 0x1C000FE44, request_mdl 0x1C0010404, query_sparse_file 0x1C001059C, create_hard_link 0x1C00106AC, set_directory_DACL 0x1C0010300) through a filter communication port, and ProcessMessage (0x1C0012B80) does not re-check the caller per message. Authorization is enforced at the port: Initialize (0x1C0012A80) builds the port descriptor with FltBuildDefaultSecurityDescriptor (FLT_PORT_ALL_ACCESS, 0x1C0012AAC), which restricts access to Administrators and SYSTEM. Because only privileged callers can reach the handlers, this is not a missing-authorization vulnerability for unprivileged callers. The descriptor and dispatcher are identical in both builds.
